facts: Kabukicho is a location-specific product scope.
Map usefulness depends on user intent, local context, and actionable navigation data.
assumptions: Research questions will be handed off before implementation.
Users likely need local decision support rather than a generic map.
Current objective context: Constitution v3 Freeze.
hypotheses: Higher intent, lower-friction content will outperform generic listings.
Visitors and workers need concise route and safety cues.
SEO opportunities likely cluster around intent-based local search queries.
recommendations: Prioritize validated user pain points over feature expansion.
Interview local visitors and repeat workers.
Test search intent clusters before expanding map data.
Compare nearby alternatives to isolate differentiation.

opportunities.title: Kabukicho Survival Map MVP expansion research
opportunities.problem: Users need faster decisions about local navigation and safety-relevant context.
opportunities.target_user: night-time visitors in Kabukicho who need smoking areas, toilets, and short-stay navigation support
opportunities.expected_value: Higher utility and better content targeting for local search intent.
evidence: Kabukicho is a location-specific product scope.
Higher intent, lower-friction content will outperform generic listings.
opportunities.recommended_next_action: Draft a research-backed issue for search intent and user segment validation.
